text: End-of-day isolation stood at 609 patients at 92.7% global occupancy (unchanged from SitRep46). SitRep47 published no patient-movement table, so the confirmed/suspected isolation split (216 confirmed, 393 suspected) is carried forward from SitRep46 and flagged as not freshly reported.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: From the Faits saillants, 19 patients were declared recovered in Ituri and 14 deaths were recorded among previously confirmed CTE patients (9 Ituri, 5 Nord-Kivu) during the cycle.

text: 73 new confirmed cases including 25 confirmed deaths were reported for 2026-06-30, in Ituri (69 including 22 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (4 including 3 deaths).

text: New confirmed cases were distributed across Bunia (29), Nizi (20), Rwampara (6), Mongbwalu (6), Komanda (2), Nyankunde (1), Tchomia (1), Mandima (1), Nia-Nia (1), Kambala (1) and Lolwa (1) in Ituri, and Katwa (2) and Beni (2) in Nord-Kivu.

text: A new affected health zone, Lolwa (Ituri), was reported on 2026-06-30, raising affected zones to 36/104; Ituri now reports 24/36 zones touched.

text: Investigations of 29 June events notified two confirmed cases in Wamba ZS (Haut-Uele province; one death and one living case that fled from Nia-Nia) and one confirmed case in Tshopo province (a 24-year-old pregnant woman who died at PK 51, the Nia-Nia epicenter, whose body was moved clandestinely to Kisangani where a sample tested positive). All were attached to and counted within the Nia-Nia health zone (Abanagomu health area), Ituri.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: 14 additional deaths were recorded among previously confirmed CTE patients (9 in Ituri: 5 CTE Bunia, 2 CTE Mongbwalu, 1 CT Bambu, 1 CTE Elikya; 5 in Nord-Kivu: 4 CTE Katwa, 1 CTE Beni), bringing confirmed deaths to 438. 19 patients recovered in Ituri, bringing DRC cumulative recoveries to 208.

text: 1,234 alerts were reported and 1,035 investigated, producing an 83.9% investigation rate; Ituri 52.1% (212/407), Nord-Kivu 100.0% (814/814), Sud-Kivu 69.2% (9/13).

text: 301 suspected cases of the day were identified, including 90 deceased validated alerts.

text: 11,646 contacts were under follow-up and 9,605 were seen, producing 82.5% follow-up coverage (completeness 90.6%; Gety and Mandima in Ituri and Musienene in Nord-Kivu had no data at consolidation).
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: 389 new contacts were listed (353 across 8 Ituri health zones and 36 across 2 Nord-Kivu health zones); 104 contacts completed 21-day follow-up (83 in Ituri, 31 in Nord-Kivu). A multidisciplinary team was deployed to Tshopo and Haut-Uele and some contacts were transferred to Nia-Nia for follow-up continuity.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: reticence_post_mortem - Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities. - Response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: case_load_and_capacity - Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation cited at 138.8% in the challenge table (SitRep47 published no patient-movement table; the banner reports national isolation occupancy 92.7%), raising nosocomial-transmission risk. - Response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: contact_followup_gap - Contact follow-up performance reached 82.5% (challenge table cites 81.3%) versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted. - Response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: weak_alert_reporting - Weak alert reporting in some affected health zones risks undetected, silent transmission. - Response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: unlocalized_confirmed_cases - Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction. - Response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: essential_medicines_gap - Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management. - Response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: ipc_and_hcw_infections - Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk; the challenge table reports 96 health-worker infections and 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%). - Response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: lab_backlog - A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 54.2% provincial CFR (challenge table). - Response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: ambulance_isolation_gap - Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk. - Response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: coordination_gap - Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions. - Response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: financial_resource_gap - A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars. - Response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: insecurity_access - Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones. - Response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: essential_services_continuity - Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality. - Response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: population_mobility - High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion, underscored this cycle by imported cases in Tshopo and Haut-Uele traced back to Nia-Nia. - Response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Source review / Before zone-level attribution use: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones; keep Tshopo/Haut-Uele imported cases counted within Nia-Nia
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.

text: Case management / Urgent: Relieve isolation load at 92.7% national occupancy and the Nord-Kivu 59.2% CFR; close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers
public_note: Reviewed derived narrative from the INSP SitRep promotion; page-11 contact details are intentionally excluded; source redistribution terms require INSP attribution and confirmation before external republication.
